content_en:
Sunan an-Nasai is known for its particularly strong content on ritual purification and prayer, areas where an-Nasai's exacting standards produced a collection of high authenticity. As-Suyuti's commentary on these sections reflects both the importance of these topics and his ability to provide useful guidance to readers.

The hadiths on water and its categories open the purification section. An-Nasai transmitted several narrations clarifying the types of water permitted for use in ablution and distinguishing pure water (tahir) from purifying water (mutahhir). As-Suyuti's commentary explains the legal implications of these distinctions, noting the Shafi'i position (which as-Suyuti himself followed as a member of the Shafi'i school) while presenting other views fairly.

The section on menstruation (hayd) contains some of the most carefully transmitted narrations in the Sunan, and as-Suyuti's commentary here demonstrates his precision. He explains the technical vocabulary — the difference between hayd (menstruation), nifas (post-natal bleeding), and istihadah (irregular bleeding) — and the different legal consequences attached to each. These distinctions have profound practical implications for women's performance of prayer and fasting.

On the description of prayer, an-Nasai's Sunan contains detailed narrations specifying the actions and words of each part of the prayer. As-Suyuti's commentary explains the Arabic of the supplications recited during prayer — the opening supplication (istiftah), the recitation of Surah al-Fatihah, and the various invocations of the bowing and prostration positions — and discusses which elements are considered obligatory and which recommended.

The chapters on the Friday prayer (Jumu'ah) and the congregational prayer are treated with particular care. As-Suyuti addresses the hadiths specifying the virtues of Friday, the obligation of the Friday prayer for free adult male Muslims, the conditions for its validity (minimum number of participants, the two khutbahs), and the adab of preparing for it through bathing, wearing fine clothes, and coming early.

An-Nasai's sections on the prayer of fear (salah al-khawf), the eclipse prayer (salah al-khusuf), and the rain prayer (salah al-istisqa') are among the most detailed treatments of these topics in the Sunan literature, and as-Suyuti's commentary clarifies the different forms these prayers may take according to the varying circumstances described in the narrations.
content_id:
Sunan al-Nasa'i dikenal karena kandungannya yang sangat kuat tentang bersuci ritual dan salat, bidang-bidang di mana standar ketat al-Nasa'i menghasilkan koleksi dengan keotentikan tinggi. Syarah al-Suyuti atas bagian-bagian ini mencerminkan pentingnya topik-topik ini sekaligus kemampuannya untuk memberikan panduan yang berguna kepada para pembaca.

Hadis-hadis tentang air dan kategori-kategorinya membuka bagian bersuci. Al-Nasa'i meneruskan beberapa riwayat yang menjelaskan jenis-jenis air yang diizinkan untuk digunakan dalam wudu dan membedakan air yang suci (thahir) dari air yang mensucikan (muthahhir). Syarah al-Suyuti menjelaskan implikasi-implikasi hukum dari perbedaan-perbedaan ini, mencatat posisi Syafi'i (yang diikuti al-Suyuti sendiri sebagai anggota mazhab Syafi'i) sambil menyajikan pandangan-pandangan lain secara adil.

Bagian tentang haid mengandung beberapa riwayat yang paling cermat ditransmisikan dalam Sunan, dan syarah al-Suyuti di sini menunjukkan ketepatannya. Ia menjelaskan kosakata teknis — perbedaan antara haid, nifas (darah pasca-melahirkan), dan istihadah (perdarahan tidak teratur) — dan konsekuensi-konsekuensi hukum berbeda yang melekat pada masing-masing. Perbedaan-perbedaan ini memiliki implikasi praktis yang mendalam bagi pelaksanaan salat dan puasa perempuan.

Tentang gambaran salat, Sunan al-Nasa'i mengandung riwayat-riwayat rinci yang menentukan tindakan-tindakan dan kata-kata di setiap bagian salat. Syarah al-Suyuti menjelaskan bahasa Arab dari doa-doa yang dibaca selama salat — doa pembuka (istiftah), bacaan Surah al-Fatihah, dan berbagai doa dalam posisi ruku dan sujud — dan membahas elemen-elemen mana yang dianggap wajib dan mana yang dianjurkan.

Bab-bab tentang salat Jumat dan salat berjamaah diperlakukan dengan perhatian khusus. Al-Suyuti membahas hadis-hadis yang menentukan keutamaan-keutamaan hari Jumat, kewajiban salat Jumat bagi Muslim laki-laki dewasa yang merdeka, syarat-syarat keabsahannya (jumlah minimum peserta, dua khutbah), dan adab mempersiapkan diri untuk itu melalui mandi, mengenakan pakaian terbaik, dan datang lebih awal.

Bagian-bagian al-Nasa'i tentang salat khauf, salat gerhana (salat khusuf), dan salat istisqa' termasuk di antara perlakuan-perlakuan paling rinci tentang topik-topik ini dalam literatur Sunan, dan syarah al-Suyuti menjelaskan berbagai bentuk yang dapat diambil salat-salat ini sesuai dengan keadaan-keadaan yang bervariasi yang digambarkan dalam riwayat-riwayat.
content_ar: Sunan an-النسائي is known for its particularly strong content on ritual purification and الصلاة, areas where an-النسائي's exacting standards produced a collection of high authenticity. As-Suyuti's commentary on these sections reflects both the importance of these topics and his ability to provide useful الهداية to readers. The hadiths on water and its categories open the purification الفصل. An-النسائي transmitted several narrations clarifying the types of water permitted for use in ablution and distinguishing pure water (tahir) from purifying water (mutahhir). As-Suyuti's commentary explains the legal implications of these distinctions, noting the الشافعي position (which as-Suyuti himself followed as a member of the الشافعي school) بينما presenting other views fairly. The الفصل on menstruation (hayd) contains some of the most carefully transmitted narrations in the Sunan, and as-Suyuti's commentary here demonstrates his precision. He explains the technical vocabulary — the difference between hayd (menstruation), nifas (post-natal bleeding), and istihadah (irregular bleeding) — and the different legal consequences attached to each. These distinctions have profound practical implications for women's performance of الصلاة and الصيام. On the description of الصلاة, an-النسائي's Sunan contains detailed narrations specifying the actions and words of each part of the الصلاة. As-Suyuti's commentary explains the Arabic of the supplications recited خلال الصلاة — the opening الدعاء (istiftah), the التلاوة of السورة al-Fatihah, and the various invocations of the bowing and prostration positions — and discusses which elements are considered obligatory and which recommended. The الأبواب on the Friday الصلاة (الجمعة) and the congregational الصلاة are treated with particular care. As-Suyuti addresses the hadiths specifying the الفضائل of Friday, the الالتزام of the Friday الصلاة for free adult male المسلمون, the conditions for its validity (minimum number of participants, the two khutbahs), and the adab of preparing for it through bathing, wearing fine clothes, and coming early. An-النسائي's sections on the الصلاة of الخوف (الصلاة al-khawf), the eclipse الصلاة (الصلاة al-khusuf), and the rain الصلاة (الصلاة al-istisqa') are among the most detailed treatments of these topics in the Sunan literature, and as-Suyuti's commentary clarifies the different forms these الصلوات may take وفقاً لـ the varying circumstances described in the narrations.
